data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-28T01:33:39.618Z</current_datetime>

I have this data-driven test using object arrays. Is there a better way?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about improving their MSTest data-driven test that uses `object[]`. The main issue is the lack of type safety with `object[]`. MSTest has a better approach using `DynamicData` with tuples or, even better, the `[DataRow]` attribute for simple inline data.
